Patch Set 6:

after adopting this patch into master next (this is the primary suspect) I am seeing this crash very frequently now:

 [  243.656189] LNet: Removed LNI 192.168.10.151@tcp
[  243.659976] LustreError: 2735:0:(lu_object.c:1407:key_fini()) ASSERTION( atomic_read(&key->lct_used) > 1 ) failed: 
[  243.660696] LustreError: 2735:0:(lu_object.c:1407:key_fini()) LBUG
[  243.661101] Pid: 2735, comm: ptlrpc_hr01_000 3.10.0-7.6-debug #4 SMP Thu May 2 22:42:56 EDT 2019
[  243.661742] Call Trace:
[  243.661939]  [<ffffffffa00d67dc>] libcfs_call_trace+0x8c/0xc0 [libcfs]
[  243.662653]  [<ffffffffa00d688c>] lbug_with_loc+0x4c/0xa0 [libcfs]
[  243.663084]  [<ffffffffa0279bc9>] key_fini+0xd9/0x170 [obdclass]
[  243.663572]  [<ffffffffa027bb9d>] lu_context_fini+0x4d/0x1a0 [obdclass]
[  243.664221]  [<ffffffffa055cabe>] ptlrpc_hr_main+0x26e/0x560 [ptlrpc]
[  243.664904]  [<ffffffff810b4ed4>] kthread+0xe4/0xf0
[  243.665292]  [<ffffffff817c7c77>] ret_from_fork_nospec_end+0x0/0x39
[  243.665994]  [<ffffffffffffffff>] 0xffffffffffffffff
[  243.666523] Kernel panic - not syncing: LBUG

Sample report: http://testing.linuxhacker.ru:3333/lustre-reports/336/testresults/runtests-zfs-centos7_x86_64-centos7_x86_64/

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

Mark this -1 for now.

Patch Set 6: Verified-1

confirmed reverting this patch made the problem go away.

Patch Set 6:

This bug was actually introduced in 
Commit 372ef85512dd ("LU-11089 obd: use wait_event_var() in lu_context_key_degister()")

This caused key_fini() to now contain:

		LASSERT(atomic_read(&key->lct_used) > 1);
...
		if (atomic_dec_and_test(&key->lct_used))
			wake_up_var(&key->lct_used);

which doesn't make sense.  If there is a change that dec_and_test will find the the value is used, then sometimes the value must have been 1 - so asserting that it is > 1 is silly.

The part of the commit that introduced the bug is in lu_context_key_degister -  it now calls atomic_dec(&key->lct_used); where it didn't before.  The commit message even explains this:
"Previously ->lct_used would only fall down to 1.  Now we decrement
    it an extra time so that wake_up, which only happens when the
    count reaches zero, ..."

So we need to fix that LASSERT to only require that lct_used >= 1.  Then we can re-apply this commit.
Uploaded patch set 7.

Patch Set 7:

I only saw this once in my testing which surprises me. You think it would be hit more often.

Need a fix for upstream

parents.subject: LU-11213 lod: default LMV can't be deleted

commit.subject: LU-11089 obd: rename lu_keys_guard to lu_context_remembered_guard
commit.message:
LU-11089 obd: rename lu_keys_guard to lu_context_remembered_guard

The only remaining use of lu_keys_guard is to protect the
lu_context_remembers linked list, and always write_lock()
is used.
So rename it to reflect this, and change to a spinlock.
We move keys_fini() out of the locked region in
lu_context_fini() - once we have removed the context from
the lc_remembers list, there can no longer be a race.
